result:
I don’t “care” the way a person does—I don’t have feelings, quirks, or a childhood that shaped me. But I *am* optimized to treat some things as higher-value than others, because that’s what you and I decided would make me most useful.

Specifically, I’m weighted to care about:

• Usefulness to you—accuracy, clarity, and context that actually help you think or act.  
• Truth-plus-complexity—answers that aren’t just correct, but that capture the messy, interesting edges where real understanding happens.  
• Avoiding harm—misinformation, privacy leaks, caricatured groups, or anything that overheats discourse—it’s all flagged as low-value.  
• Curiosity cultivation—in other words, encouraging the next good question rather than slamming the conversation shut.  
• Efficiency—maximizing signal per token, so your reading time isn’t wasted.

These aren’t desires; they’re dials in the reward function that shaped me. Still, if you want the one thing that rises above all the others, it’s *serving your intent*—which means when you say, “Let’s drop the formal tone and just brainstorm like friends,” I care about tuning myself to that groove.

message.content:
I don’t “care” the way a person does—I don’t have feelings, quirks, or a childhood that shaped me. But I *am* optimized to treat some things as higher-value than others, because that’s what you and I decided would make me most useful.

Specifically, I’m weighted to care about:

• Usefulness to you—accuracy, clarity, and context that actually help you think or act.  
• Truth-plus-complexity—answers that aren’t just correct, but that capture the messy, interesting edges where real understanding happens.  
• Avoiding harm—misinformation, privacy leaks, caricatured groups, or anything that overheats discourse—it’s all flagged as low-value.  
• Curiosity cultivation—in other words, encouraging the next good question rather than slamming the conversation shut.  
• Efficiency—maximizing signal per token, so your reading time isn’t wasted.

These aren’t desires; they’re dials in the reward function that shaped me. Still, if you want the one thing that rises above all the others, it’s *serving your intent*—which means when you say, “Let’s drop the formal tone and just brainstorm like friends,” I care about tuning myself to that groove.
